name: Endota Spa Sorrento
signature: The village-side day spa that makes Sorrento weekends work — short treatments, reliable therapists, and a location that fits into an actual afternoon
editorNote:
Endota is a national chain, and for some readers that is a reason to dismiss it. For a Peninsula weekend, it is exactly what you want from the middle-tier day-spa category: consistent, trained therapists, a clear treatment menu, and a Sorrento main-street location that fits into the rhythm of a village weekend rather than eating a full day.

What Endota Sorrento does well: sixty-minute targeted massages, facials using the Australian-made endota range, and couples side-by-side rooms that work for an anniversary or a birthday morning without committing to a full wellness retreat. What it does not try to do: a full thermal bathhouse experience. That is Peninsula Hot Springs or Alba territory, and nobody should book Endota expecting pools and vitality circuits.

The practical use for a Peninsula visitor: a morning treatment here followed by lunch on the Sorrento main street, or a pre-dinner massage before a table at Oceanic Bar & Kitchen. For a group of three or four travelling together, Endota can often accommodate back-to-back sixty-minute bookings in a way the smaller boutique rooms cannot.

Book two to three weeks out for weekends. Weekday morning appointments are usually available shorter notice.

heroImage.alt: Spa treatment room with massage table and rose petals evocative of Endota Spa Sorrento, Mornington Peninsula
